user_prompt: First, I want a `Diagnostics::memory_check_in` function that measures worst case free stack and free heap from within the call, this information is cached for display later. It will be inserted into many poll and pump calls, and into things that are event handlers or request handlers. make sure it is thread safe, as both CPUs and many threads will call into it
we need to track in `void FlyGui::poll()` how many times `bool drawn = redraw(false);` is true. Have it call into `Diagnostics::gui_drew()` if `drawn` is true
I also want to see how often `exceeded_threshold` is true inside `void update_write_duration_stats(uint32_t duration_us)`

I also want to track how many overflow events. To `AudioFifo`, add a counter for both overflow and underflow events, an event is going from normal to overflow  or normal to underflow states. Have `Diagnostics` report this as a rate per second.

Have a `Diagnostics::poll()` function, it doesn’t poll much, it might have its own `memory_check_in` , but more importantly, every 1 second, it emits a diagnostic message to serial port with the memory data and the frame rate (so it needs to reset the counter for the draw calls and for the long-write).
agent_reply: Added `Diagnostics::memory_check_in`, `memory_stats`, `gui_drew`, `long_write_exceeded`, and `poll` in [diagnostics.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/diagnostics.h:9) / [diagnostics.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/diagnostics.cpp:37). The counters/minimums use atomics, and `Diagnostics::poll()` emits one serial line per second with worst observed free stack/heap, GUI draw FPS, long-write rate, FIFO overflow rate, and FIFO underflow rate.

commit_message: diagnostics, fixed a bug with speaker peak decay

user_prompt: calling `tools\code-style-format.ps1`  blows up my github desktop list of changes with a ton of changes but no content changes, is it just line endings?
agent_reply: - `.gitattributes` forces `* text=auto eol=crlf`, so some LF/CRLF noise is real. Git warned specifically about:

So the smaller files are mostly whitespace/formatting noise, but `src/sprites.cpp` is genuine huge churn from `clang-format`. The formatter script runs over all `.c/.cpp/.h/.hpp` under `src`, `include`, `lib`, and `test`, and `src/sprites.cpp` is generated/enormous, so clang-format rewrites a mountain of it.
`src/version.c` is just the generated version hash changing from the build.

Short version: GitHub Desktop’s “tons of changes” is partly line-ending/whitespace, but the big offender is `src/sprites.cpp` being formatted. That file probably wants to be excluded from `tools\code-style-format.ps1`, same as the third-party ignored dirs.

user_prompt: quickly review the changes to platformio.ini I made to enable the display of warnings
agent_reply: - Medium: [platformio.ini](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:77) changing `DBG_LOG_LOCAL_LEVEL` from `DBG_LOG_ERROR` to `DBG_LOG_WARN` also makes [conf.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:84) define `BUILD_IS_DEBUG`. That enables the periodic diagnostics `Serial.printf` block in [diagnostics.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/diagnostics.cpp:99). If you only wanted warnings during this upload debugging session, that side effect is worth knowing.

- Low: [platformio.ini](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:75) also bumps `CORE_DEBUG_LEVEL` and `LOG_LOCAL_LEVEL` to `ESP_LOG_WARN`. That can surface Arduino/ESP-IDF/library warnings in addition to your `DBG_LOGW` calls, so serial output may get noisier than just the filesystem diagnostics.
No mechanical issue with the change itself: it should display your `DBG_LOGW()` warnings because `DBG_LOG_USE_SERIAL=1` and the local level is now `WARN`.
